items.back: Prevent collisions involving aircraft operating in the system.
Not optional; provide them when the work situation permits.
Separate aircraft and issue safety alerts as required.
Use automation procedures over nonautomation procedures when workload, communications, and equipment capabilities permit.
Use radar separation over nonradar when operationally advantageous and feasible; use nonradar when the situation dictates.
First come, first served as circumstances permit, except listed priority situations and without compromising safety.
Only when expeditious compliance is required to avoid an imminent situation.
Only when prompt compliance is required to avoid development of an imminent situation; a later non-expedite altitude restatement cancels the expedite instruction.
Issue when aware an aircraft is in unsafe proximity to terrain, obstructions, or other aircraft.

items.front: Safety alert after pilot reports corrective action
items.back: Once the pilot says action is being taken, further alerts may be discontinued.

items.front: Inflight equipment malfunction report: first controller task
items.back: Determine the nature and extent of any special handling desired.
Pilot cannot accept undue delay after reaching destination; it is not itself an emergency declaration.
Inform any facility receiving control jurisdiction and be alert for delays.
Information about NAS components or flight conditions that may adversely affect air safety; report it as soon as possible to the appropriate facility or office.
Request a report from a second aircraft.
Continue use, inform the first aircraft, and record the incident.
Military command prerogative; not invoked indiscriminately by individual units or pilots.
May only be applied to specified military IFR operations documented in an LOA or other appropriate FAA/military document.
Additions, modifications, and exceptions used when a common procedure has not been attained or to meet a specific requirement.
Control the formation as a single aircraft.
Flight leader and pilots are responsible for separation within the formation, including join-up and breakaway.
Ensure necessary coordination has been accomplished first.

items.front: Transfer of control can occur at...
items.back: A prescribed or coordinated location, time, fix, or altitude; or after radar handoff and frequency change when authorized by directive/LOA.

items.front: Surface area clearance involving nonapproach control tower
items.back: Coordinate with the appropriate tower on an individual aircraft basis unless an LOA specifies otherwise.
Transfer before the aircraft enters the receiving controller's jurisdiction unless otherwise coordinated or specified.
Restate the request in complete or abbreviated terms followed by APPROVED; APPROVED AS REQUESTED may replace a lengthy readback.
An aircraft is operating behind another aircraft and wake turbulence separation is required.
Position, altitude if known, and direction of flight.
Issue to aircraft on your frequency when proximity may diminish to less than applicable separation minima; where no minima apply, issue when proximity warrants.
Position, distance, course, type of unmanned aircraft, and altitude when known.
When it appears pilot actions constitute a pilot deviation, notify the pilot workload permitting.
(Identification) POSSIBLE PILOT DEVIATION ADVISE YOU CONTACT (facility) AT (telephone number).
FL 290 through FL 410 inclusive.
Eligible aircraft are separated vertically by 1,000 feet in RVSM airspace.
